creator: jsatkinson@embarqmail.com
text:
Matthew D. Fuller wrote:
> Yeesh, you sleep a little late one day...    8-}
> 

I was a) bored, b) there. ;)

> The patch in the pr de-sorts the plist (leading to a way larger diff
> than necessary).
 >

Yeah, it added quite a few bits and pieces here and there, and removed a 
few more. I just installed to /tmp and then did `file bzrdata -type f > 
tmp-plist` then mechanically fixed it from there. This seemed both 
faster and less prone to errors. I realize now, I should have passed it 
through sort too... oops. Sorry.

 > Also, some launchpad changes somewhere along the
 > line make it not work quite right.  Here's an updated patch that will
 > do the trick:
 >

I don't understand what you mean here. I have no issues fetching:

mpehisto# make distclean fetch checksum
===>  Cleaning for bazaar-ng-1.13
===>  Deleting distfiles for bazaar-ng-1.13
=> bzr-1.13.tar.gz doesn't seem to exist in /usr/ports/distfiles/.
=> Attempting to fetch from https://launchpad.net/bzr/1.13/1.13/+download/.
bzr-1.13.tar.gz                               100% of 3895 kB  374 kBps 
00m00s
=> MD5 Checksum OK for bzr-1.13.tar.gz.
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for bzr-1.13.tar.gz.

Maybe both should be used so that it has two and quiets portlint?
